page: Microsoft Azure D3_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv2 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and utilizes a dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz. The configuration includes 4 physical cores with 4 vCPUs, reflecting a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The system is equipped with 14.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.5 GB per core. It features 205 GB of local storage and does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying virtualization is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark evaluations place the D3_v2 in the lower performance tiers for compute-heavy operations. The single-core stress-ng performance score of 823.19 and the single-threaded gzip compression rate of 17.84 GB/sec fall into the bottom 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is also limited, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 3027.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 4476.89, both categorized in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block read and write operations (40.69 GB/sec and 31.3 GB/sec respectively), while small-block read bandwidth remains weak at 295.8 GB/sec.

Given the hardware profile and benchmark results, the D3_v2 presents a clear tradeoff between dedicated resource allocation and raw processing speed. While it features dedicated physical cores and a substantial 205 GB local storage allocation, its computational throughput is constrained, as evidenced by weak database operations (2126.87 on Passmark) and low text processing scores. This virtual machine is suited for light general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, or small-scale development environments that require dedicated CPU cores but do not demand high-performance computing or intensive memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure D3_v2 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor clocked at 2.6 GHz, paired with 14.0 GB of RAM and 205 GB of local storage. No GPUs are included. Performance benchmarks indicate that the D3_v2 sits primarily in the lower tiers, with single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 10%, and multi-core performance categorized in the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth is average, reaching 40.69 GB/sec for reads and 31.3 GB/sec for writes. Due to these performance constraints, this instance is suited for light general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and small-scale development tasks where dedicated CPU allocation is required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D3_v2 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M cores, 14.0 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, suited for light workloads.

tagline: A general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and local storage for basic, non-intensive computing workloads.

14.0 GB of system memory and 205 GB of local storage
